title: Nigritella × eggeriana
intro: Nigritella × eggeriana, also known as Egger's Nigritella, is a hybrid orchid species found in the Alps, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers and distinctive shape. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe respiratory issues and boost the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Nigritella × eggeriana include iridoid glycosides, such as nigritelline and nigritin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and may be used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Nigritella × eggeriana is a hybrid orchid species. It belongs to the genus Nigritella and the family Orchidaceae. This species is a result of the cross between two other Nigritella species, making it a natural hybrid. The classification of Nigritella × eggeriana is within the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Orchideae. Its classification as a hybrid orchid species is crucial for understanding its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
